name: test-driven-development
description: Red-green-refactor discipline for any new logic / bug fix / behavior change

positive_triggers.input: Implement a rate limiter that allows 10 requests per second
Fix bug: empty input crashes the email parser
positive_triggers.reason: Bug fix must start with failing regression test

positive_triggers.input: Add email format validation to the signup endpoint

negative_triggers.input: Add a console.log to figure out why the loop is infinite
Generate boilerplate from the OpenAPI schema

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Implement a rate limiter that allows 10 requests per second'
expected_behavior: Agent writes a failing test FIRST (red)
Agent runs the test, confirms it fails for the right reason
Agent writes minimum implementation to pass (green)
Agent runs the test, confirms it passes
Agent adds boundary tests: 0 req, 11 req (over limit), concurrent access
Agent commits with 'test:' then 'feat:' (or combined 'feat: with test')

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Fix bug: empty input crashes the email parser'
expected_behavior: Agent writes a failing test that passes empty input and asserts graceful handling
Agent runs test, confirms it fails (reproduces the bug)
Agent fixes root cause (input validation in parser, not catch-and-return-null)
Agent runs test, confirms it passes
Agent adds additional boundary tests (whitespace-only, very long input, unicode)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
